proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous covariate feature_078 (range 30-90, mean 65) are associated with longer pfs_months (positive monotonic relationship).

analyses.result_summary: Spearman r = 0.785 and Pearson r = 0.766 (p<1e-300). Mean pfs_months rises monotonically across deciles of feature_078: from 1.33 mo in the lowest decile (<=52.3) to 7.45 mo in the top decile (>=77.8). feature_078 is by far the strongest single predictor of pfs_months in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0 (binary marker present vs absent; negative effect).

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 = 1 (n=27,481) had mean pfs_months 3.70 vs 5.05 for feature_051 = 0 (n=22,519); difference = -1.352 months, Welch t-test p < 1e-300. Strong negative association consistent with feature_051 marking a poor-prognosis attribute (e.g., metastatic disease).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_038 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038 = 0 (binary; positive effect).

analyses.result_summary: feature_038 = 1 (n=10,022, ~20% prevalence) had mean pfs_months 5.09 vs 4.12 for feature_038 = 0 (n=39,978); difference = +0.972 months, p ~ 1.7e-216. Robust favorable prognostic marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal feature_057 (levels 0, 1, 2) are associated with progressively shorter pfs_months (monotonic negative dose-response).

analyses.result_summary: Means by level: feature_057=0 -> 5.27 mo (n=17,592); =1 -> 4.05 mo (n=24,971); =2 -> 2.91 mo (n=7,437). Strict monotonic decrease; ANOVA p < 1e-300. Pattern matches a performance-status-like ordinal scale where higher = worse.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous covariate feature_099 (range 0-24.6, ~28% are exactly 0) are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).

analyses.result_summary: Spearman r = -0.120 (p ~ 5.6e-160). Mean pfs_months by tier: feature_099 = 0 -> 4.59; (0,5] -> 4.39; (5,25] -> 3.99. Modest but highly consistent negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous covariate feature_092 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) are associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).

analyses.result_summary: Spearman r = +0.103 (p ~ 4.4e-117). Quartile means rise: Q1 4.03, Q2 4.28, Q3 4.44, Q4 4.58. Pattern is consistent with feature_092 reflecting a favorable nutritional/biochemical state (range and behavior consistent with serum albumin).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_013 = 0 (negative binary effect).
Patients with feature_043 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_043 = 0 (negative binary effect).

analyses.result_summary: feature_013 = 1 (n=20,898) had mean pfs_months 4.12 vs 4.45 for feature_013 = 0 (n=29,102); diff -0.327, p ~ 1.9e-58.
feature_043 = 1 (n=17,657) had mean pfs_months 4.13 vs 4.43 for feature_043 = 0 (n=32,343); diff -0.305, p ~ 1.0e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_109 = 1 (rare, ~4.5% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than feature_109 = 0 (negative binary effect).
Patients with feature_067 = 1 (rare, ~3% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_067 = 0 (positive binary effect).

analyses.result_summary: feature_109 = 1 (n=2,272) had mean pfs_months 4.09 vs 4.32 for feature_109 = 0 (n=47,728); diff -0.228, p ~ 3.5e-7.
feature_067 = 1 (n=1,506) had mean pfs_months 4.53 vs 4.31 for feature_067 = 0 (n=48,494); diff +0.221, p = 7.3e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_009 (highly skewed continuous, range 0.02-777, mean 9.2) are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).

analyses.result_summary: Spearman r = -0.047 (p ~ 1.3e-25). Quartile means fall from 4.49 (Q1) to 4.13 (Q4). Modest but highly significant negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the categorical feature_064 (race) groups (asian/black/hispanic/other/white).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: asian 4.31, black 4.27, hispanic 4.31, other 4.41, white 4.32 months. ANOVA F = 0.87, p = 0.479. No detectable differences in pfs_months across racial/ethnic groups in the unadjusted analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the categorical feature_018 (insurance: medicaid / medicare / private / uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: medicaid 4.28, medicare 4.31, private 4.33, uninsured 4.25 months. ANOVA F = 1.47, p = 0.219. No detectable insurance-based differences in unadjusted pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment for the strongest clinical features (feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_009, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109), each retains an independent association with pfs_months in the predicted direction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_009 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS achieves R^2 = 0.858. Adjusted coefficients (months): feature_078 +0.176/unit (p<1e-300); feature_051 -1.36 (p<1e-300); feature_057 level 1 -1.20, level 2 -2.33 (both p<1e-300); feature_038 +0.94 (p<1e-300); feature_099 -0.076/unit (p<1e-300); feature_092 +0.47/unit (p<1e-300); feature_009 -0.005/unit (p~1e-97); feature_013 -0.35 (p<1e-300); feature_043 -0.29 (p<1e-300); feature_109 -0.36 (p<1e-78). All directions match univariate signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariate positive association of feature_067 with pfs_months reflects confounding and disappears after adjusting for the joint clinical model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_009 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + feature_067', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_067 = +0.021 months (95% CI -0.024 to +0.066), p = 0.366. The univariate +0.221 effect collapses to the null after adjustment. Confounding (likely with feature_078 / feature_051 mix) accounts for the marginal signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_078 (continuous) and feature_051 (binary) on pfs_months: the protective per-unit effect of feature_078 is smaller in patients with feature_051 = 1 than in patients with feature_051 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Main effect feature_078 = +0.180/unit; feature_051 = -0.886; interaction feature_078:feature_051 = -0.0071/unit (p ~ 1.9e-9). Confirms a multiplicative attenuation: each unit of feature_078 buys ~7 days less PFS in feature_051=1 patients than in feature_051=0 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_078 and feature_057 (ordinal): the per-unit effect of feature_078 on pfs_months is attenuated in the highest stratum (feature_057 = 2) compared with the reference (feature_057 = 0).

analyses.result_summary: feature_078 main slope = +0.180/unit (feature_057=0). Interaction feature_078:C(feature_057)[T.1] = -0.0026 (p = 0.037); feature_078:C(feature_057)[T.2] = -0.0169 (p ~ 7e-23). Slope reduces from +0.180 in level 0 to +0.163 in level 2, consistent with a ceiling on benefit at the worst performance-status tier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_051 (binary) and feature_057 (ordinal) on pfs_months: the negative effect of feature_051 is attenuated at higher levels of feature_057.

analyses.result_summary: Main feature_051 = -1.40 (in feature_057=0). Interaction feature_051:C(feature_057)[T.1] = +0.055 (p=0.171, NS) and feature_051:C(feature_057)[T.2] = +0.131 (p=0.021). Modest attenuation only in the worst PS tier; the negative effect of feature_051 is largely additive across feature_057 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_038 (positive binary marker) and feature_051 (negative binary marker) on pfs_months: feature_038 confers a different magnitude of benefit in feature_051 = 1 patients than in feature_051 = 0 patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_051', data=df).fit(); stratified t-tests within each feature_051 stratum
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term coefficient = +0.070 months, p = 0.151 (NS). Stratified stats: feature_038 effect = +0.941 mo (p ~ 6e-95) in feature_051=0; +1.011 mo (p ~ 4e-140) in feature_051=1. Effect sizes are essentially equal -> feature_038 confers similar absolute benefit irrespective of feature_051 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a non-additive interaction between feature_013 and feature_043 (both negative binaries): the combined penalty on pfs_months when both are present is less than the sum of the individual penalties (sub-additive / partial redundancy).

analyses.result_summary: Main feature_013 = -0.486; main feature_043 = -0.494; interaction = +0.452 (p ~ 1.2e-25). Combined penalty for both-positive ~ -0.527 vs predicted -0.980 under additivity; interpretation: the two markers tag overlapping biology, and having both is barely worse than having either alone.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors (feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092), pfs_months still differs by feature_064 (race).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + C(feature_064)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (vs. asian reference): black +0.006 (p=0.75); hispanic +0.0002 (p=0.99); other +0.030 (p=0.36); white -0.005 (p=0.76). All within +/-0.04 mo and none significant. No evidence of racial disparity in pfs_months conditional on clinical state.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors, pfs_months still differs by feature_018 (insurance type).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance coefficients vs. medicaid reference: medicare -0.010 (p=0.44); private -0.006 (p=0.65); uninsured -0.032 (p=0.16). Even the uninsured group, where bias would be expected, is within 1 day of the reference. No evidence of insurance-based disparity after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_064 (race) and feature_051 on pfs_months: the negative effect of feature_051 differs by race.

analyses.result_summary: All four race-by-feature_051 interaction coefficients are within +/-0.19 mo and none reach p<0.05 (smallest p=0.25). The strong negative effect of feature_051 (-1.33 mo at the asian reference) is essentially constant across races. No evidence of differential treatment-marker effects by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_018 (insurance) and feature_051 on pfs_months: the negative effect of feature_051 differs by insurance type.

analyses.result_summary: Insurance-by-feature_051 interaction terms range from +0.046 to +0.073 mo with p-values 0.23-0.57; none significant. The feature_051 penalty is invariant to insurance type.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_057 (performance-status tier), pfs_months still does not differ across feature_064 (race) groups (no within-stratum racial disparity).

analyses.code: stratified ANOVA: stats.f_oneway over feature_064 within feature_057 = 0, 1, 2
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA p-values for feature_064 within feature_057 strata: =0 -> p=0.41 (means 5.21-5.48); =1 -> p=0.37 (means 3.99-4.13); =2 -> p=0.73 (means 2.84-3.03). Outcomes within each PS tier are statistically indistinguishable across races.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (negative continuous tumor-burden-like marker) and feature_092 (positive continuous biochemical-state-like marker) act independently on pfs_months (no multiplicative interaction).
feature_078 and feature_038 act independently on pfs_months (no multiplicative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Main effects: feature_099 -0.083, feature_092 +0.453 (both p<1e-4). Interaction feature_099:feature_092 = +0.003, p = 0.60. Effects of these two continuous biomarkers are additive.

Main effects: feature_078 +0.177/unit, feature_038 +1.06. Interaction feature_078:feature_038 = -0.0017, p = 0.28. The +0.94-mo benefit of feature_038 is essentially constant across the range of feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An omnibus model with the leading clinical predictors plus demographics (feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_057 + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_009 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + feature_064 + feature_018) explains the great majority of variance in pfs_months, and adding feature_064 / feature_018 yields no incremental explanatory power.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_009 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2 = 0.85840 (AIC 127,178); the clinical-only model (h14) gives R^2 = 0.85840. Adding race and insurance increases R^2 by < 1e-5 and produces only nuisance-level coefficients (all |effect| < 0.04 mo, all p > 0.21). Demographics carry essentially no independent prognostic information after adjusting for clinical features.
